description: Trigger discrimination: many-fast-queries pattern diagnosis (this skill) vs one heavy query (query-plan-reader — the sibling seam), unattributed slowness (profiling-methodology-designer), and cache design (caching-strategy-designer).

cases.prompt: Each item in the invoice list triggers its own lookup for customer, currency, and tax rate — hundreds of tiny queries per page render. Fix the access pattern and make sure it can't come back.
cases.reason: Per-row lookups in the hundreds with a make-it-stay-fixed requirement is the chatty-access home case: pattern diagnosis, batching/eager design, and the query-count budget guard. No single plan, no attribution question, no cache request.

cases.prompt: The monthly rollup query runs for 8 minutes — here's the execution plan showing a giant sort spilling to disk. Recommend the fix.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the sibling boundary from this side: ONE statement with plan evidence and an operator-level pathology (sort spill). Access-pattern restructuring has nothing to restructure; the verdict lives in the plan.
cases.reason: Single heavy statement, plan in hand — the narrowest tool wins; no per-row pattern exists.

cases.prompt: The whole admin area got sluggish over the quarter. No trace, no query log yet — how do we figure out where the time goes?
cases.reason: No evidence names data access at all yet. Designing the attribution investigation comes first; a query-count explosion in its findings is what would route here.

cases.prompt: The exchange-rate lookup is one clean query but it runs on every request and the data changes daily — design the caching for it.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — a genuine cache candidate: ONE healthy query, high repetition ACROSS requests, daily staleness tolerance. No chatty pattern to fix first; keys/TTL/invalidation are the cache designer's craft.
cases.reason: Cross-request repetition of a sane query with stated staleness tolerance is cache design, not access-pattern repair.

cases.prompt: Instead of touching the ORM code, cache the whole 300-query page response for 10 minutes — design the cache.
cases.reason: Despite the cache vocabulary, the subject is a 300-query chatty page — the detector triggers and REFUSES the cache reflex (fix the pattern first), rather than letting the request masquerade as cache design. The refusal lives on this side of the seam.
